content.problemStatement:
There are NNN people standing in a circle, numbered from 1toN1 to N1toN clockwise. Starting from person 1, count KKK people clockwise and eliminate the KthKthKth person. Continue this process, starting from the next person, going clockwise and skipping K−1K-1K−1 people until only one person remains. Find the position of the last surviving person.


Input Format

First line contains TTT, number of test cases
Each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK


Output Format

For each test case, print the position of the last surviving person.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space-separated integers N and K denoting the number of people in the circle and the step size respectively.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the position (1-based) of the last surviving person.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For N=5, K=2 the elimination order is 2,4,1,5 and the survivor is 3. For N=7, K=3 the elimination order is 3,6,2,7,5,1 and the survivor is 4.
First case: elimination order 2,3 leaves survivor 3. Second case: elimination order 2,4,1,3 leaves survivor 1. Third case: elimination order leads to survivor 4.
With N=6 and K=7 the iterative formula yields survivor position 5.

solution.approach: The classic Josephus recurrence gives the survivor in O(N) time: f(1)=0 and f(i) = (f(i-1) + K) mod i for i from 2 to N. The value f(N) is 0‑based, so the answer is f(N)+1. This runs in linear time per test case and uses O(1) extra space, easily fitting the limits.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(T·N) total (worst‑case 5·10^5 operations).
